name: Senolytics coverage repair assessment
summary: The senolytics track has an auditable vertical slice across preclinical D+Q discovery/healthspan evidence, early human biomarker and pilot human studies, a phase 2 randomized bone trial, a 2025 cognition/mobility pilot, translation-review context, risk-of-bias records, and registry-only active trial-watch records. DKD and AD-risk human records now include PubMed abstract-located sample, dosing, timepoint, and effect details, and IPF has an abstract-located dosing-completion result plus corrected RCT sample size. These upgrades remain below source-snapshot-backed extraction grade, and the D+Q bone publication-table and supplement reconciliation remains incomplete.

evidence_categories.rationale: Includes early D+Q human biomarker, randomized IPF pilot, phase 2 bone, and AD-risk cognition/mobility pilot records; DKD, IPF, and AD-risk now have at least abstract-located extraction for sample, dosing, timing, or effect details.
Includes foundational discovery work plus the old-mouse healthspan and post-treatment survival paper.
Cellular senescence targeting is anchored by the discovery source and translation review, but pathway-level extraction is not yet complete.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Future extraction should separate senescent-cell survival pathways, SASP markers, and tissue-specific clearance evidence.

evidence_categories.rationale: The IPF, bone, and AD-risk records explicitly encode mixed, pilot, primary/subgroup, and feasibility limitations instead of flattening them into positive claims; the AD-risk record now includes the abstract-level MoCA and TNF-alpha estimates.

Safety-oriented records now include the D+Q bone registry aggregate counts, full-text adverse-event table extraction, an accepted structured headache preferred-term row, and retained locators for withdrawal, dosing cadence, and durability context. The remaining publication table rows, discontinuation details, and the DKD/IPF/AD-risk human safety slices still need row-level or source-specific extraction before safety synthesis.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Run bounded extraction for the remaining D+Q bone adverse-event rows and discontinuation counts, then ingest or retain source snapshots for DKD, IPF, and AD-risk safety extraction before attempting cross-human safety synthesis.

evidence_categories.rationale: Two active no-results trial-watch records were added, but the full senolytics registry surface still needs a broader surveillance pass.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Add deferred fisetin frailty registries and associated no-results status records in the next surveillance pass.

evidence_categories.rationale: The current slice includes a narrative translation review but no systematic-review or meta-analysis record.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Search specifically for recent systematic reviews, trial reviews, and evidence maps.

evidence_categories.rationale: Records separate senolytic mechanism/intervention class from evidence tier, clinical indication, and registry status, reducing the risk that the track becomes an unsupported efficacy bucket.

known_gaps.description: DKD and AD-risk human studies now have PubMed abstract-located sample, dosing, timepoint, and effect details, and IPF has an abstract-located dosing-completion result plus corrected randomized-pilot sample size. However, durable source snapshots could not be created in this worker, the legacy IPF generic outcome/result still needs reconciliation, and the D+Q bone RCT still needs full publication table and supplement reconciliation.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Create or refresh durable PubMed/PMC source snapshots for DKD, IPF, and AD-risk, complete full-text/table extraction where needed, and reconcile the bone RCT registry extraction against full article tables and supplements.

known_gaps.description: Aggregate adverse-event counts and the D+Q bone headache preferred-term row have been extracted for the D+Q bone RCT, and retained full-text locators now identify the safety table, withdrawal narrative, dosing cadence, and durability discussion. Event-specific structured rows beyond headache, discontinuation count fields, and source-snapshot-backed safety extraction for the DKD, IPF, and AD-risk human studies remain incomplete.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Run table-row extraction context packs for the remaining D+Q bone adverse-event terms and withdrawals, then ingest or retain appropriate source snapshots before extending safety extraction across DKD, IPF, and AD-risk studies.

known_gaps.description: Only two active no-results registry records were added in this repair pass; broader fisetin and D+Q trial surveillance remains incomplete.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Run a surveillance pass over ClinicalTrials.gov for senolytics, D+Q, fisetin, and senescence-targeting terms.

known_gaps.description: No recent systematic-review or meta-analysis source has been screened into the knowledge base.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Search for recent systematic reviews, meta-analyses, and trial-landscape reviews.

next_coverage_action: Create source-snapshot-backed full-text extraction passes for DKD, IPF, and AD-risk, reconcile the legacy IPF generic outcome/result against article tables, and continue bounded D+Q bone publication-table and supplement reconciliation before any cross-human synthesis.

provenance.note: Original triage-level coverage anchor for the senolytics vertical slice.
Retained full-text adverse-event table lists overall events and preferred-term rows for control and D+Q arms, with blank cells requiring zero/not-reported caution.
Retained full text provides withdrawal/discontinuation details, D+Q dosing cadence over 20 weeks, and durability limitations for short-term versus 20-week bone-turnover effects.
ClinicalTrials.gov retained registry snapshot provides aggregate serious adverse event, other adverse event, and death counts through approximately 20 weeks.
